meaning: n.(由鼹鼠打洞扒出的泥土堆成的)鼹鼠丘,无意义的事,小困难(或障碍)
examples: The press, making a mountain out of a molehill, precipitated an unnecessary economic crisis.
Don't make a mountain out of molehill.
The giants of management education have laboured mightily to bring forth a molehill.
From a spending standpoint, we already went off the fiscal molehill and it was fine.
Cosmology, in other words, is a mountain of theory built on a molehill of evidence.
